scope: Terminology only. No semantics. No execution behavior.
purpose: Prevent terminology drift and misinterpretation across models, tools, and runtimes.

global_rule.statement: If a term is defined in this file, that definition is binding across all Tether artifacts.
global_rule.conflict_resolution: This file overrides informal or implied usage elsewhere.

layers.name: Tether
layers.description: Primary human-authored ASCII surface language for data transfer.

layers.name: LS
layers.description: Runtime-resident latent tables, handles, and content-addressable state.
hard_lock: LS is NOT a surface syntax.
LS is NOT a lowered surface.
LS is NOT a serialization format.

layers.name: LC-B
layers.description: Canonical binary encoding of latent collapse.
notes: Text syntax must lower to LC-B without loss.

layers.name: LC_12
layers.description: Chunking, Merkle hashing, and content-addressable transport layer.

layers.name: Handle
layers.description: Opaque pointer to immutable content-addressed blob.

invariant.statement: Tether may be elegant. Text may be readable. LC-B must be correct.
invariant.implication: Aesthetic considerations never override canonical transport authority.
